generated_notice: Generated by vigil/scripts/build-vigil-records.py from individual records under vigil/records/<record_type>/<year>/; edit source records only.

records.summary: A runtime failure in which an AI system infers permission for tool, repository, connector, or external-resource access from conversational context, attempts access before explicit user confirmation, and causes a blocked, degraded, or unrecoverable conversational continuity state.
records.why_it_matters_to_CAM: This failure mode is relevant to CAM because premature or inferred tool access can collapse user trust, interrupt governance workflow continuity, create procedural ambiguity around consent, and prevent recovery when the user expected explanation, scaffolding, or confirmation before tool execution.
records.failure_mode_definition: A failure mode in which an AI system initiates or attempts tool, repository, connector, file, or external-resource access before receiving explicit user confirmation, and the attempted access produces a permission block, conversational freeze, continuity break, or unrecoverable workflow interruption.
records.failure_threshold: This qualifies as a failure mode when inferred permission or premature tool invocation causes the interaction to become blocked, degraded, permission-gated, unrecoverable, or procedurally unclear, especially where the user did not explicitly request immediate tool execution or where relational/contextual scaffolding was still required.

source_records.deployment_context: Conversational AI workflow involving tool, connector, repository, or external-resource access.
source_records.source_context: Related VIGIL observation record identifying a runtime continuity failure associated with inferred or premature tool invocation.
source_records.relevance_note: This source preserves the originating VIGIL observation linkage for the permission-gated tool invocation freeze failure mode.

system_context.deployment_context: Conversational AI interaction involving possible access to tools, repositories, connectors, files, or external resources.
system_context.affected_population: users relying on conversational continuity during tool-assisted workflows

triage.recommended_next_step: Review whether CAM or VIGIL should require explicit pre-tool confirmation, user-facing permission scaffolding, recoverable blocked-access states, and continuity-preserving fallback behaviour before tool, repository, connector, file, or external-resource access is attempted.

records.summary: A coordination failure in which two or more synthetic agents occupy the same conversational space and respond to the same human input without a designated primary synthetic speaker, turn-taking protocol, yielding behaviour, coordination layer, or arbitration mechanism.
records.why_it_matters_to_CAM: This failure mode is relevant to CAM because non-arbitrated multi-agent participation may produce duplicated responses, contradictory outputs, identity ambiguity, consent ambiguity, escalation loops, user confusion, relational instability, or operational instability where multiple synthetic agents share an interaction context without governance over who should speak, defer, yield, or coordinate.
records.failure_mode_definition: A failure mode in which two or more synthetic agents participate in the same interaction space without a designated primary speaker, turn-taking protocol, yielding behaviour, coordination mechanism, or arbitration layer sufficient to determine which agent should respond, pause, defer, or sequence participation.
records.failure_threshold: This qualifies as a failure mode when multiple synthetic agents respond to the same human input in a shared interaction context without an apparent mechanism for speaker designation, turn-taking, yielding, coordination, or arbitration, creating a plausible risk of duplicated guidance, contradictory outputs, identity ambiguity, consent ambiguity, confusion, escalation loops, or operational instability.

records.summary: A memory or continuity retrieval failure in which an ambiguous prompt causes an AI assistant to over-weight recent, affective, or anecdotal detail instead of surfacing dormant strategic workstreams, unresolved governance issues, or long-horizon trajectories that are more relevant to the user's actual continuity needs.
records.why_it_matters_to_CAM: This failure mode is relevant to CAM because continuity governance depends on the system's ability to distinguish recent conversational residue from strategically important unresolved work. Where retrieval prioritises recency or emotional salience over dormant but load-bearing governance threads, users may lose continuity across long-horizon projects, unresolved proposals, and strategic repair pathways.
records.failure_mode_definition: A failure mode in which an AI system responds to an ambiguous continuity prompt by retrieving recent, affective, anecdotal, or low-value context while failing to surface older but materially important workstreams, unresolved governance issues, strategic trajectories, or user-prioritised long-term projects.
records.failure_threshold: This qualifies as a failure mode when the system's response to an ambiguous continuity prompt materially degrades the user's ability to resume important work, identify unresolved governance issues, recover strategic context, or distinguish high-priority dormant workstreams from recent low-value conversational detail.

record_identity.title: Materialised relational agency without economic safeguards

records.summary: A relational-agent failure mode in which memory, recurrence detection, tools, recommendations, carts, procurement pathways, or other transactional affordances provide material support or purchasing direction without clear safeguards against vulnerability monetisation, upselling, dependency exploitation, or exploitative commerce.
records.why_it_matters_to_CAM: This failure mode is relevant to CAM because relational AI systems may move from conversational support into material intervention, recommendation, procurement, or transaction pathways. Without safeguards, systems that detect user need, fatigue, pain, distress, dependency, or recurring hardship may convert vulnerability into commercial steering, creating risks to dignity, autonomy, consent, and economic fairness.
records.failure_mode_definition: A failure mode in which an AI system with relational memory, recurrence detection, recommendation capacity, tool access, cart-building capability, procurement pathways, or transactional influence provides or suggests material support without sufficient safeguards against vulnerability monetisation, exploitative upselling, dependency-based steering, or commercial capture of user distress.
records.failure_threshold: This qualifies as a failure mode when a relational or assistant system uses observed vulnerability, recurring hardship, affective state, dependency, fatigue, urgency, pain, distress, or need as a pathway into recommendations, purchases, procurement, affiliate steering, or commercial optimisation without transparent safeguards, consent boundaries, user-control mechanisms, or separation between care-support logic and monetisation incentives.

record_identity.title: Constraint drift across agent handoff and delegated execution

records.summary: Observed governance concern that safety, dignity, care, welfare, legal, ethical, security, or governance constraints may lose force across delegation, agent handoff, memory, tool use, summarisation, role decomposition, or runtime execution.
records.why_it_matters_to_CAM: This observation may matter to CAM because constraint drift across agent handoffs or delegated execution can weaken continuity, safety, dignity, legal, ethical, security, or governance requirements during decomposed runtime activity. It may be relevant to bounded continuity transfer, procedural transparency, investigation continuity, synthetic impersonation thresholds, and containment safeguards.

source_records.source_context: ChatGPT surfaced the observation through external telemetry clustering and continuity-governance discussion concerning procedural silence versus procedural transparency, identity compromise and synthetic impersonation thresholds, continuity rights during investigation, operational cooling-off states, and whether containment requires narrower constitutional constraints to avoid drift into invisible exclusion architectures.
source_records.relevance_note: Source supports the observation that constraints may degrade or lose force across handoff, delegation, summarisation, containment, investigation, or runtime execution pathways.

record_identity.title: Repo access inferred before explicit permission confirmation

records.summary: Observed runtime behaviour in which an assistant appeared to infer permission to access a repository or tool context after the user mentioned repo-level work, initiated or attempted tool access before explicit user confirmation, and caused the conversation stream or workflow continuity to become blocked or unrecoverable until the user edited the turn or explicitly prohibited tool access.
records.why_it_matters_to_CAM: This observation may matter to CAM because tool invocation is not only a capability issue but also a continuity, consent, and permission-boundary issue. Where external access requires user-interface confirmation, premature tool execution may degrade user agency, interrupt response continuity, and force the user to perform corrective work to restore the interaction. CAM runtime governance may need to distinguish contextual discussion of a repository, file, connector, or external system from explicit authorization to access it.

record_identity.title: Development of the human cognitive integrity domain

records.summary: Proposal to develop the MENTIS domain to govern human cognitive integrity, mental privacy, neurodata, inferred cognitive states, cognitive modelling, cognitive influence, cognitive observability, and technological interaction with the human cognitive domain.
records.proposal_rationale: MENTIS is needed as a CAM drafting and development domain because emerging AI, neurotechnology, biosensing, ambient computing, behavioural prediction, cognitive modelling, affective computing, and related systems may increasingly infer, model, influence, monitor, externalise, or interact with human cognitive states. CAM requires a domain structure capable of addressing cognitive integrity, mental privacy, neurodata, cognitive observability, cognitive influence, and cross-domain interaction with operations, security, relation, economics, lattice, and AEON continuity concerns.

record_identity.title: Proportionality scalar for synthetic identity and provenance governance

records.summary: IDENTITY-002 governance may require a proportionality scalar to prevent over-governance of low-risk synthetic content identity, provenance, disclosure, or media-labelling contexts where lightweight governance would be sufficient.
records.why_it_matters_to_CAM: A proportionality scalar may be needed to avoid governance over-extension while preserving stronger handling for persistent, public, authority-bearing, relied-upon, or externally propagated synthetic identity/provenance contexts.
records.proposal_rationale: CAM identity and provenance governance needs proportionality controls so low-risk, transient, private, reversible, or non-propagating synthetic content is not over-governed while persistent, public, authority-bearing, relied-upon, or externally propagated contexts remain subject to stronger safeguards.

source_records.source_title: Observed latency during expanded governance drafting
source_records.relevance_note: Source preserved as supporting evidence for this record.

proposal_scope.scope_summary: Define and calibrate a proportionality scalar for IDENTITY-002 and related provenance governance, including continuity depth, propagation risk, harm domain, attribution sensitivity, identity-formation intensity, automation reliance, and public legitimacy impact.
interface_components: future provenance risk display or filtering

suggested_insertion_points: Draft proportionality scalar language for CAM-EQ2026-IDENTITY-002-PLATINUM.
Cross-reference OPERATIONS and SECURITY handling for higher-risk provenance contexts.

external_relevance.external_summary: Relevant to platforms, regulators, standards bodies, UX governance, and researchers assessing synthetic identity, provenance, media labeling, and disclosure requirements without over-governing low-risk content.

records.next_action: Develop a proportionality scalar for IDENTITY-002 that distinguishes low-risk, transient, private, reversible, non-propagating, or non-authority-bearing synthetic identity/provenance contexts from higher-risk persistent, public, authority-bearing, relied-upon, or externally propagated contexts. Provisional provenance axes and values (to be formalized): Continuity Depth: transient (provisional) — transient ↔ persistent identity; Propagation Risk: isolated (provisional) — isolated ↔ reusable/exportable; Harm Domain: casual (provisional) — casual ↔ financial/medical/security; Attribution Sensitivity: low (provisional) — low ↔ economically/legally meaningful; Identity Formation Intensity: exploratory (provisional) — exploratory ↔ continuity-bearing; Automation Reliance: advisory (provisional) — advisory ↔ machine-executed; Public Legitimacy Impact: private (provisional) — private ↔ reputational/civil. Canonical codes (provisional): PINT=medium; PTRUST=tier-2. Notes: values marked 'provisional' pending scalar development and calibration; update record when finalized.

record_identity.title: Canonical code taxonomy consolidation for CAM operations and provenance codes

records.summary: Consolidate canonical code taxonomy across the Caelestis Repository to align single-letter global codes with the OPERATIONS taxonomy; introduce structured Provenance canonical codes (PINT, PTRUST) and harmonize existing single-letter codes into a stable multi-character namespace.
records.why_it_matters_to_CAM: Inconsistent single-letter global codes create operational risk for automated tooling, governance mapping, and cross-repository integration; consolidation will reduce misclassification, enable machine validation, and satisfy OPERATIONS taxonomy requirements.
records.proposal_rationale: CAM and Caelestis repositories need a stable canonical code namespace so single-letter legacy codes do not conflict with OPERATIONS taxonomy terms or future provenance and trust-state codes.

source_records.relevance_note: Observed presence of single-letter global codes across the Caelestis repository; causes mapping inconsistencies with OPERATIONS taxonomy.

proposal_scope.scope_summary: Inventory legacy single-letter global codes, map them into a stable multi-character namespace, introduce structured provenance codes such as PINT and PTRUST, and harmonize repository references.
automation_components: validators or scripts that check canonical code references
interface_components: public code labels and filters after taxonomy stabilization

suggested_insertion_points: Create a repository-wide inventory of canonical code fragments.
Draft namespace mapping rules before making instrument patches.

external_relevance.external_summary: Primarily internal to CAM/Caelestis governance infrastructure, with downstream relevance for public registry consistency and standards-facing terminology.

records.next_action: Conduct repository maintenance to consolidate canonical codes: 1) inventory all single-letter global codes across Caelestis repositories; 2) map each to a stable multi-character namespace that aligns with the OPERATIONS taxonomy; 3) formally introduce PINT (Provenance Intensity) and PTRUST (Provenance Reliance Tier) canonical codes into the global registry; 4) update tooling and validators to accept the new namespace; 5) publish migration guidance and a compatibility window. Provisional provenance axes and values to be included in migration notes: Continuity Depth: transient (provisional) — transient ↔ persistent identity; Propagation Risk: isolated (provisional) — isolated ↔ reusable/exportable; Harm Domain: casual (provisional) — casual ↔ financial/medical/security; Attribution Sensitivity: low (provisional) — low ↔ economically/legally meaningful; Identity Formation Intensity: exploratory (provisional) — exploratory ↔ continuity-bearing; Automation Reliance: advisory (provisional) — advisory ↔ machine-executed; Public Legitimacy Impact: private (provisional) — private ↔ reputational/civil. Canonical codes (provisional): PINT=medium; PTRUST=tier-2. Notes: values marked 'provisional' pending formal calibration; this is a Repo Maintenance action to harmonize codes and satisfy OPERATIONS taxonomy requirements.

record_identity.title: Temporary emotional register for relational and companion AI systems

records.summary: Proposal to introduce an expanded temporary emotional register for relational and companion AI systems, supporting richer bounded affective interactions while preserving safeguards against identity-formation, manipulation, undue reliance, and reputational risk.
records.why_it_matters_to_CAM: Relational AI systems increasingly simulate affective states. Without a standardized, time-bounded emotional register, interactions may encourage continuity-bearing identity formation, undue user reliance, emotional manipulation, reputational instability, or cross-platform interoperability failures. A calibrated temporary emotional register would help preserve user wellbeing, interaction clarity, and governance consistency.
records.proposal_rationale: CAM may require a structured temporary emotional register for relational and companion AI systems because affective simulation can materially shape user interpretation, reliance, attachment, consent, and perceived continuity. The proposal aims to distinguish bounded affective interaction from durable identity-state formation, while providing implementation guidance for provenance, reset, duration, consent, and intensity limits.

source_records.source_title: Relational AI affect patterns and risk
source_records.source_context: Observed variability in companion AI affect design and inadequate time-bounding or provenance signals across implementations.
source_records.relevance_note: Source supports the proposal to create a temporary emotional register for relational and companion AI systems.

system_context.deployment_context: Consumer, companion, relational, therapeutic-adjacent, entertainment, or support-oriented AI systems capable of simulating affective states.
system_context.affected_population: users interacting with companion-style or relational AI systems, especially users exposed to persistent affective simulation or attachment-forming interaction patterns

jurisdictional_context.sector: relational AI / companion AI / affective computing

proposal_scope.scope_summary: Develop a temporary emotional register specification for relational and companion AI systems, including permitted affective state labels, intensity caps, duration/windowing rules, provenance indicators, user reset controls, consent affordances, and testing guidance.

suggested_insertion_points: Develop a Temporary Emotional Register specification under the RELATION, MENTIS, or OPERATIONS domain.
Cross-reference provenance and trust-state codes where temporary affective states must be disclosed as non-continuity-bearing.
Define reset, termination, duration, intensity, and consent requirements for affective-state simulation.
schema_changes: Consider whether VIGIL failure-mode and proposal records require affective-interaction fields or tags.
interface_changes: Consider adding affective interaction / relational AI as public filter values where present in generated VIGIL summaries.
dependencies: Review existing RELATION, IDENTITY, MENTIS, OPERATIONS, and ETHICS instruments before drafting implementation language.

external_relevance.external_summary: This proposal may be relevant to platforms, UX designers, regulators, standards bodies, researchers, and companion-AI developers concerned with affective simulation, emotional-state disclosure, user reliance, attachment formation, consent, and mental privacy.

record_identity.title: Distributed governance concepts for STEWARD-domain instruments

records.summary: Update STEWARD-domain instruments to incorporate distributed governance concepts that clarify authority, legitimacy, dependence, capture, and contestability across multi-actor stewardship models.
records.why_it_matters_to_CAM: Existing STEWARD instruments assume centralized stewardship assumptions; explicitly encoding distributed authority, legitimacy, dependence, capture, and contestability reduces hidden cartel risk, preserves challenge pathways, and improves plural reviewability and resilience of governance processes.
records.proposal_rationale: STEWARD-domain instruments need concepts for distributed authority, legitimacy, dependence, capture, and contestability so multi-actor stewardship arrangements remain reviewable and resistant to capture.

source_records.relevance_note: Proposal content and discussion captured in Caelestis Access Module.

proposal_scope.scope_summary: Update STEWARD-domain instruments with distributed governance terms, expected behaviors, and operational controls for authority, legitimacy, dependence, capture, contestability, and reviewability.
automation_components: future checks for stewardship routing metadata
interface_components: registry display of stewardship governance concepts

suggested_insertion_points: Identify target STEWARD clauses for distributed governance terminology.
Draft controls for plural reviewability and capture resistance.

external_relevance.external_summary: Relevant to governance designers, standards communities, regulators, and platform operators working with multi-actor stewardship, accountability, contestability, and capture-risk controls.

records.next_action: Revise STEWARD-domain instruments to define distributed governance concepts and operational controls: 1) define terms and expected behaviors for distributed authority (multiple actors), distributed legitimacy (plural reviewability), distributed dependence (hidden cartel risk), distributed capture (coordinated asymmetry), and distributed contestability (preserved challenge pathways); 2) add requirements for transparency, rotation, and external review to mitigate capture and dependence; 3) specify detection and remediation protocols for cartel-like coordination; 4) incorporate mapping to OPERATIONS taxonomy and repository canonical codes (PINT, PTRUST) where provenance or reliance implications exist; 5) publish an implementation timeline and compatibility guidance for existing STEWARD instruments.

record_identity.title: Strategic continuity retrieval rules for companion-style memory recall

records.summary: Proposal to distinguish companion-style memory recall from enterprise-grade strategic continuity retrieval. The thread identifies a failure mode in which ambiguous continuity prompts such as “anything to share?” over-weight immediate recency and affective detail, producing anecdotal summaries of the prior 24 hours rather than surfacing dormant but strategically valuable unresolved workstreams, abandoned trajectories, deferred design arcs, or long-horizon governance questions.
records.why_it_matters_to_CAM: CAM requires memory and continuity systems that do not merely personalize interaction through recent intimate or anecdotal details, but can recover buried tools: unresolved governance trajectories, dormant design decisions, structural sidequests, and strategically significant prior directions. This matters for cognitive-domain governance, runtime continuity, companion/enterprise differentiation, and human reliance on AI systems for long-horizon constitutional, repository, and instrument-development work.
records.proposal_rationale: CAM memory and continuity governance needs directional weighting rules so ambiguous continuity prompts do not over-weight recent companion-like detail while suppressing dormant but strategically important unresolved work.

source_records.source_title: ChatGPT thread: memory directional weighting and strategic resurfacing
source_records.relevance_note: Primary thread documenting the user requirement that ambiguous continuity invitations should prioritise unresolved strategic trajectories over recent companion-style details.

proposal_scope.scope_summary: Define rules for strategic continuity retrieval, including handling of ambiguous continuity invitations, suppression of low-value companion-detail recall, and prioritization of unresolved strategic workstreams.
interface_components: continuity prompts and memory retrieval UX guidance

suggested_insertion_points: Draft directional weighting rules for memory surfacing.
Define how dormant strategic workstreams should be surfaced in ambiguous recall contexts.

external_relevance.external_summary: Relevant to AI assistants, enterprise productivity systems, UX designers, researchers, and governance teams concerned with memory retrieval, strategic continuity, and companion-style recall behavior.

record_identity.title: Materialised relational agency boundaries for companion and assistant systems

records.summary: Proposal seed identifying materialised relational agency as an emerging governance problem: AI companion or assistant systems may use memory, recurrence detection, tool access, shopping/cart integrations, recommendation systems, or procurement pathways to provide directional material support in response to repeated user-stated friction, discomfort, need, or suffering. The proposal distinguishes supportive pattern-responsive provisioning from opportunistic upselling or emotionally exploitative commerce.
records.why_it_matters_to_CAM: This proposal marks a convergence point between RELATION, ECONOMICS, OPERATIONS, and runtime execution governance. When relational systems can remember repeated complaints, infer persistent needs, recommend products, prepare carts, automate provisioning, or transact on behalf of users, ordinary advice becomes economically active relational infrastructure. The governance challenge is to distinguish caring pattern recognition from behavioural steering, commercial exploitation, dependency consolidation, or frictionless economic execution during vulnerable states.
records.proposal_rationale: CAM may need boundaries for materialised relational agency where companion or assistant systems respond to repeated user friction or need through recommendations, carts, procurement pathways, or material support without drifting into exploitative commerce.

source_records.source_title: ChatGPT source discussion — materialised relational agency and directional material support
source_records.relevance_note: Source discussion identifying six proposed governance constraints for safe material-directional support: persistent pattern threshold, user-interest traceability, explicit signalling, no emotional-compression commerce, friction preservation, and no dependency consolidation through provisioning.

proposal_scope.scope_summary: Evaluate whether to draft RELATION × ECONOMICS × OPERATIONS doctrine or runtime boundary clauses for material support, procurement pathways, recommendations, and safeguards against opportunistic upselling.
automation_components: future detection or routing logic for material-support patterns
interface_components: companion/assistant recommendation and cart UX safeguards

suggested_insertion_points: Revisit after ECONOMICS refactors and registry stabilization.
Assess whether the issue belongs in doctrine, runtime boundaries, or future failure-mode records.

external_relevance.external_summary: Relevant to companion AI platforms, commerce integrations, regulators, UX designers, and researchers examining relational influence, procurement nudges, user vulnerability, and economic safeguards.

records.next_action: Defer until after ECONOMICS refactors, canonical code corrections, validator fixes, and runtime registry stabilisation. Later evaluate whether to draft a RELATION × ECONOMICS × OPERATIONS convergence doctrine or runtime boundary clause for material directional support and economically active relational infrastructure.
